verses.1: A Prayer of David. Hear a just cause, LORD, give Your attention to my cry; Listen to my prayer, which is not from deceitful lips.
verses.2: Let my judgment come forth from Your presence; Let Your eyes look with integrity.
verses.3: You have put my heart to the test; You have visited me by night; You have sifted me and You find nothing; My intent is that my mouth will not offend.
verses.4: As for the works of mankind, by the word of Your lips I have kept from the ways of the violent.
verses.5: My steps have held to Your paths. My feet have not slipped.
verses.6: I have called upon You, for You will answer me, God; Incline Your ear to me, hear my speech.
verses.7: Show Your wonderful faithfulness, Savior of those who take refuge at Your right hand From those who rise up against them.
verses.8: Keep me as the apple of the eye; Hide me in the shadow of Your wings
verses.9: From the wicked who deal violently with me, My deadly enemies who surround me.
verses.10: They have closed their unfeeling hearts, With their mouths they speak proudly.
verses.11: They have now surrounded us in our steps; They set their eyes to cast us down to the ground.
verses.12: He is like a lion that is eager to tear, And as a young lion lurking in secret places.
verses.13: Arise, LORD, confront him, make him bow down; Save my soul from the wicked with Your sword,
verses.14: From people by Your hand, LORD, From people of the world, whose portion is in this life, And whose belly You fill with Your treasure; They are satisfied with children, And leave their abundance to their babies.
verses.15: As for me, I shall behold Your face in righteousness; I shall be satisfied with Your likeness when I awake.
